question.text: What is a CAS (Consolidated Account Statement) in the context of mutual fund investments?

answer.text: A statement showing only mutual fund holdings from a single AMC
A single statement consolidating an investor's mutual fund and demat holdings across all AMCs, sent monthly if transactions occurred or half-yearly otherwise
A statement issued only on investor request through the AMC
A statement showing only the capital gains and tax details of investments

explanation.text: **The Logic:** CAS is a Consolidated Account Statement that provides a unified view of an investor's mutual fund holdings across ALL AMCs, along with demat holdings, in a single document. It is generated by the RTAs (CAMS and KFintech) and sent monthly if any transaction occurred during the month, or half-yearly if no transactions took place. It can be sent via email and/or physical copy. **The Trap:** CAS is not AMC-specific — that's an individual account statement from each AMC. CAS consolidates across ALL AMCs, making it a powerful portfolio-level document. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A covers CAS features; Series II-B tests the RTA's role in generating and distributing CAS as a key investor service. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Use the CAS as a portfolio review tool during client meetings — it shows the complete MF picture in one place. If a client says 'I don't know all my investments,' the CAS is the answer. Ensure clients have updated email addresses for timely CAS delivery.
